kids.question: Who wrote the letter to Philemon?
kids.explanation: Philemon 1:1 — "Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother, unto Philemon."

kids.question: What was the name of the runaway servant Paul was sending back?
kids.explanation: Philemon 1:10 — "I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my bonds."

kids.question: What did Paul ask Philemon to do with Onesimus?
kids.option_a: Receive him back as a beloved brother
kids.explanation: Philemon 1:16 — "receive him… Not now as a servant, but above a servant, a brother beloved."

teens.question: Where was Paul when he wrote the letter to Philemon?
teens.explanation: Philemon 1:1,9 — "Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ" and "being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ."

teens.question: How does Paul describe Onesimus before and after his conversion?
teens.option_a: Once unprofitable but now profitable to both Paul and Philemon
teens.option_c: A skilled craftsman who lost his way
teens.option_d: A wealthy man who became poor
teens.explanation: Philemon 1:11 — "Which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me."

teens.question: Rather than commanding Philemon, what approach does Paul take?
teens.option_a: He appeals to him on the basis of love
teens.option_d: He asks the other apostles to intervene
teens.explanation: Philemon 1:8-9 — "though I might be much bold in Christ to enjoin thee… Yet for love's sake I rather beseech thee."

teens.question: What does Paul offer to do regarding any debt Onesimus owes?
teens.option_a: Paul says "put that on mine account" — he will repay it
teens.option_b: He asks the church to take up a collection
teens.option_c: He says the debt should be forgiven without payment
teens.option_d: He says Onesimus should work it off
teens.explanation: Philemon 1:18 — "If he hath wronged thee, or oweth thee ought, put that on mine account."

teens.question: What does Paul say Philemon's love and faith have done?
teens.option_a: Refreshed the hearts (bowels) of the saints
teens.explanation: Philemon 1:7 — "we have great joy and consolation in thy love, because the bowels of the saints are refreshed by thee, brother."

adults.question: What does Paul call Philemon in his greeting?
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:1 — "unto Philemon our dearly beloved, and fellowlabourer."

adults.question: Who else is addressed in the salutation besides Philemon?
adults.option_a: Apphia (beloved), Archippus (fellowsoldier), and the church in Philemon's house
adults.option_c: The entire church of Colossae by name
adults.option_d: The Roman governor of the province
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:2 — "And to our beloved Apphia, and Archippus our fellowsoldier, and to the church in thy house."

adults.question: What does Paul pray regarding Philemon's faith?
adults.option_a: "That the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus"
adults.option_b: "That thy faith fail not in times of trouble"
adults.option_d: "That thy faith may convert the emperor"
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:6 — "That the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus."

adults.question: What is the meaning of the name "Onesimus" and how does Paul play on it?
adults.option_a: It means "useful/profitable" — Paul says he was once unprofitable but is now profitable, a wordplay on his name
adults.option_b: It means "faithful" — Paul says he proved his name true
adults.option_c: It means "servant" — Paul says he should be freed from that identity
adults.option_d: It means "blessed" — Paul says he has been a blessing
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:10-11 — Onesimus means "useful"; Paul writes "in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable."

adults.question: What subtle reminder does Paul give Philemon in verse 19?
adults.option_a: "I Paul have written it with mine own hand, I will repay it: albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides"
adults.option_b: "Remember that I am an apostle with authority over you"
adults.option_c: "Remember the day I healed your mother"
adults.option_d: "Remember that the Lord watches all transactions"
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:19 — Paul reminds Philemon that he owes Paul his very salvation — a gentle but powerful persuasion.

adults.question: What does Paul suggest may have been the providential reason for Onesimus's departure?
adults.option_a: "Perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever" — temporary separation for eternal gain
adults.option_b: God sent him away as punishment
adults.option_c: Onesimus needed to learn a trade in Rome
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:15 — "For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever."

adults.question: What confidence does Paul express about Philemon's response?
adults.option_a: "Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say"
adults.option_b: "I know you will do the minimum required"
adults.option_c: "I expect you to follow my direct command"
adults.option_d: "I am uncertain whether you will obey"
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:21 — "Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say."

adults.question: What practical request does Paul make at the end of the letter?
adults.option_a: Prepare a lodging for him, as he hopes to be granted to them through their prayers
adults.option_b: Send money for his legal defense
adults.option_d: Send Onesimus back to Rome permanently
adults.explanation: Philemon 1:22 — "But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your prayers I shall be given unto you."
